mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, causing negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.5pp over 3 months, invalidating the short-term bearish signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns when RSAFS and HOUST both rise, with a hit rate below 50% over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates expanding corporate output and capacity utilization, which translates to higher earnings expectations and positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to generate positive 20-day forward returns during easing cycles, with a mean return difference indistinguishable from zero (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation triggers expectations of tighter monetary policy, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL increases consistently coincide with positive 5-day SPX returns, yielding a negative Rank IC below -0.02 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly nonfarm payroll additions above 200,000 confirm robust economic momentum and wage growth, supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS growth exceeds 200k per month for 3 consecutive months
failure_condition: SPX delivers negative 60-day returns in > 55% of periods following three consecutive months of PAYEMS growth exceeding 200,000.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent quarterly GDP expansion and monthly retail sales growth validate synchronized demand across production and consumption channels, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined GDPC1 and RSAFS expansion signal yields a 20-day forward return hit rate below 45% across multiple business cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rates during a tightening cycle restrict credit availability and increase corporate borrowing costs, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 60-day returns in > 60% of observations when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0% during tightening phases, contradicting the rate sensitivity claim.
